title: 3 Reliable Dividend Stocks With Yields Up To 3.7% For Your Portfolio
content:
The United States market has experienced a notable upswing, rising 1.9% over the last week and 24% over the past year, with earnings projected to grow by 15% annually. In this environment, reliable dividend stocks can offer investors potential stability and income, making them an attractive option for those looking to enhance their portfolios amidst ongoing market growth.

Top 10 Dividend Stocks In The United States

Name Dividend Yield Dividend Rating Provident Financial Services (PFS) 4.51% ★★★★★★ Peoples Bancorp (PEBO) 4.90% ★★★★★★ OTC Markets Group (OTCM) 5.46% ★★★★★★ Omega Healthcare Investors (OHI) 5.96% ★★★★★★ First Interstate BancSystem (FIBK) 5.62% ★★★★★★ First Community Bankshares (FCBC) 5.34% ★★★★★★ Farmers National Banc (FMNB) 5.07% ★★★★★★ Ennis (EBF) 4.61% ★★★★★★ Dillard's (DDS) 5.45% ★★★★★★ Columbia Banking System (COLB) 5.34% ★★★★★★

Click here to see the full list of 114 stocks from our Top US Dividend Stocks screener.

Let's explore several standout options from the results in the screener.

Orrstown Financial Services

Simply Wall St Dividend Rating: ★★★★★☆

Overview: Orrstown Financial Services, Inc. is the financial holding company for Orrstown Bank, offering commercial banking and financial advisory services to a diverse range of clients in the United States, with a market cap of $712 million.

Operations: Orrstown Financial Services, Inc. generates its revenue primarily through its Community Banking segment, which accounted for $252.08 million.

Dividend Yield: 3.3%

Orrstown Financial Services offers a stable and reliable dividend, with a low payout ratio of 25.2%, ensuring dividends are well covered by earnings. The company has consistently increased its dividend payments over the past decade, though its current yield of 3.29% is below the top tier in the US market. Recent financial results show strong earnings growth, supporting future dividend sustainability. Additionally, Orrstown recently completed a small share buyback and filed for a $200 million shelf registration to enhance financial flexibility.

Delve into the full analysis dividend report here for a deeper understanding of Orrstown Financial Services. The analysis detailed in our Orrstown Financial Services valuation report hints at an deflated share price compared to its estimated value.ORRF Dividend History as at Apr 2026

S&T Bancorp

Simply Wall St Dividend Rating: ★★★★★☆

Overview: S&T Bancorp, Inc. is the bank holding company for S&T Bank, offering retail and commercial banking services to consumers and businesses in Pennsylvania and Ohio, with a market cap of approximately $1.55 billion.

Operations: S&T Bancorp, Inc. generates revenue primarily through its Community Banking segment, which accounted for $394.70 million.

Story Continues

Dividend Yield: 3.4%

S&T Bancorp maintains a solid dividend profile, with a payout ratio of 39.8%, ensuring dividends are well covered by earnings. The company has consistently increased its dividend over the past decade, recently raising it to $0.36 per share. Despite a yield of 3.42% being below top-tier US payers, stable earnings growth supports future payouts. Additionally, S&T's $100 million share repurchase program highlights its commitment to enhancing shareholder value while maintaining financial strength.

Click to explore a detailed breakdown of our findings in S&T Bancorp's dividend report. Our expertly prepared valuation report S&T Bancorp implies its share price may be lower than expected.STBA Dividend History as at Apr 2026

Bank of N.T. Butterfield & Son

Simply Wall St Dividend Rating: ★★★★★☆

Overview: The Bank of N.T. Butterfield & Son Limited offers community, commercial, and private banking services to individuals and small to medium-sized businesses, with a market cap of $2.10 billion.

Operations: The Bank of N.T. Butterfield & Son Limited generates $606.79 million in revenue from its banking services segment.

Dividend Yield: 3.7%

Bank of N.T. Butterfield & Son offers a stable dividend with a 33.5% payout ratio, ensuring coverage by earnings and projected to improve to 28%. The dividend yield of 3.74% is reliable, though not among the highest in the US market. Earnings have grown consistently, supporting dividend sustainability. Recent financials show increased net income and earnings per share, while strategic buybacks and board enhancements underscore a commitment to shareholder value.

Take a closer look at Bank of N.T. Butterfield & Son's potential here in our dividend report. Upon reviewing our latest valuation report, Bank of N.T. Butterfield & Son's share price might be too pessimistic.NTB Dividend History as at Apr 2026

title: CB Financial Services And Two Other Dividend Stocks To Consider
content:

In such an environment, dividend stocks like CB Financial Services can offer investors potential income stability and growth opportunities, making them worth considering for those seeking to balance risk and reward in their portfolios.

Below we spotlight a couple of our favorites from our exclusive screener.

CB Financial Services

Simply Wall St Dividend Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: CB Financial Services, Inc. is a bank holding company for Community Bank, offering banking products and services to individuals and businesses, with a market cap of $175.27 million.

Operations: CB Financial Services, Inc.'s revenue primarily comes from its Community Banking Services segment, which generated $42.94 million.

Dividend Yield: 3.2%

CB Financial Services is trading at 32.8% below its estimated fair value, offering potential value for dividend investors. The company recently increased its quarterly dividend by 7.7% to US$0.28 per share, though the high payout ratio of 104.8% raises concerns about sustainability despite a history of stable and growing dividends over the past decade. Recent earnings improvements may support future payouts, but profit margins have decreased from last year’s levels.

Click here and access our complete dividend analysis report to understand the dynamics of CB Financial Services. Insights from our recent valuation report point to the potential overvaluation of CB Financial Services shares in the market.CBFV Dividend History as at Apr 2026

Mechanics Bancorp

Simply Wall St Dividend Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: Mechanics Bancorp, with a market cap of $3.26 billion, operates as the holding company for Mechanics Bank, offering a range of banking services including checking and savings accounts, retirement accounts, money market accounts, time certificates of deposit, and safe deposit boxes across California, Oregon, Washington, and Hawaii.

Story Continues

Operations: Mechanics Bancorp generates revenue primarily from its Community Banking segment, which accounts for $642.66 million.

Dividend Yield: 5.6%

Mechanics Bancorp's dividend yield of 5.63% places it among the top 25% of US dividend payers, supported by a low payout ratio of 16.5%, indicating strong coverage by earnings. However, its dividend history is less stable, with payments being volatile over its four-year history. Recent financial results showed significant one-off impacts on earnings, and while profits have grown substantially recently, future sustainability remains uncertain due to delayed SEC filings announced in March 2026.

Navigate through the intricacies of Mechanics Bancorp with our comprehensive dividend report here. In light of our recent valuation report, it seems possible that Mechanics Bancorp is trading beyond its estimated value.MCHB Dividend History as at Apr 2026

Dillard's

Simply Wall St Dividend Rating: ★★★★★★

Overview: Dillard's, Inc. operates retail department stores across the southeastern, southwestern, and midwestern United States with a market cap of approximately $9 billion.

Operations: Dillard's, Inc. generates its revenue primarily from Retail Operations, amounting to $6.32 billion, and Construction activities contributing $268.86 million.

Dividend Yield: 5.5%

Dillard's offers a dividend yield of 5.45%, placing it in the top 25% of US dividend payers, supported by a low payout ratio of 3%, indicating robust earnings coverage. The company's dividends have been stable and growing over the past decade, with reliable payments. Recent financial results show slight declines in revenue and net income but do not impact dividend sustainability significantly. A recent $0.30 per share cash dividend was declared, payable in May 2026.

ChoiceOne Financial Services

Overview: ChoiceOne Financial Services, Inc. is the bank holding company for ChoiceOne Bank, offering a range of banking services in Michigan with a market cap of $421.29 million.

Operations: ChoiceOne Financial Services, Inc. generates revenue primarily through its banking segment, which accounts for $146.62 million.

Dividend Yield: 4.1%

ChoiceOne Financial Services maintains a reliable dividend, recently affirming a $0.29 per share payout, slightly higher than the previous year's first quarter. Despite trading at 55.3% below estimated fair value and facing lower profit margins due to large one-off items, its dividends are well-covered by earnings with a sustainable payout ratio of 55.9%. Operations: Community Financial System, Inc.'s revenue is primarily derived from its Banking and Corporate segment at $565.44 million, followed by Employee Benefit Services at $142.36 million, Insurance at $54.43 million, and Wealth Management at $39.43 million.

Story Continues

Community Financial System offers a stable dividend, recently affirming a $0.47 per share payout with a sustainable 46.4% payout ratio, indicating solid earnings coverage. Overview: FinVolution Group is an investment holding company that operates in the online consumer finance industry across China, Indonesia, and internationally, with a market cap of approximately $1.21 billion.

Operations: FinVolution Group generates revenue primarily from its Internet Software & Services segment, which amounted to CN¥13.57 billion.

Dividend Yield: 6%

FinVolution Group's dividend yield of 6.01% ranks in the top 25% of US payers, supported by a low payout ratio of 18.3%, ensuring earnings coverage. However, its dividend history is less reliable, with volatility over seven years and no consistent growth pattern. Despite recent revenue declines and a completed $77.26 million share buyback, FinVolution increased its annual dividend to US$0.286 per share for May 2026 distribution, highlighting commitment to shareholder returns amidst fluctuating earnings.

Mercantile Bank

Mercantile Bank offers a reliable dividend yield of 3.06%, supported by a low payout ratio of 27.4%, indicating sustainability. Opera

Opera's dividend yield of 5.52% places it in the top 25% of US dividend payers, supported by a payout ratio of 66.2%.
